title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI has commoditized code generation, making requirements elicitation and stakeholder alignment the new bottleneck in software development. He presents three practical tools for this work: story mapping, a four-question value framework, and the VAD thinking path, using a VisualLabs hackathon case study where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned due to lack of business value, not technology limitations.
writeup: Balázs Horváth of VisualLabs argues that AI has commoditized code generation, shifting the bottleneck in software development to requirements elicitation and stakeholder alignment. The talk lays out three practical tools for that work: story mapping, a four-question value framework, and the VAD thinking path. The central evidence is a VisualLabs hackathon case study in which 17 of 21 agent ideas were dropped for lack of business value rather than technical limits, underscoring that defining the problem and aligning stakeholders remains the harder, less automatable task.
